Germline Variant Analysis Tutorial

Anastasiia Korosteleva

2026-02-19

Introduction

This tutorial introduces germline variant analysis using targeted panel sequencing data. We explore real-world samples from the study:

Clinical impact of a targeted next-generation sequencing gene panel for autoinflammation and vasculitis (Omoyinmi et al., PLoS ONE)

We’ll analyze annotated variants from three example patients:

  • Patient 2: chr10-70598687-G>C
  • Patient 23: PTEN c.650T>G (p.Val217Gly)
  • Patient 25: RNF213 c.12034T>C (p.Cys4012Arg)

The data were processed with the nf-core/sarek pipeline (v3.4.2) using both DeepVariant and HaplotypeCaller, with annotations from VEP (Variant Effect Predictor).

Nextflow

NXF_VER=24.04.4 nextflow run nf-core/sarek -r 3.4.2 \
-profile docker \
--input /samplesheet.csv \
--outdir /results \
--genome GATK.GRCh38 \
--wes \
--save_mapped \
--save_output_as_bam \
--multiqc_title tutorial_example2 \
--trim_fastq  \
--tools mutect2,manta,vep \
-bg  \
-resume

Objectives

By the end of this session, you will be able to:

  1. Load and explore annotated VCF files.
  2. Extract and filter variants by impact or gene.
  3. Visualize the variant consequence spectrum.
  4. Interpret example variants in a clinical context.

Setup

library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

Task 0: Explore raw VCF file and VEP

  1. Explore the file format via sublime/text-reader
  2. Go to https://www.ensembl.org/Tools/VEP and explore possible fields (our reference is GRCh38.p14)
  3. Work with pre-loaded VEP.ann.vcf or your own
  4. Server-version of VEP gives more plugins and freedom in use

Task 1: Load VEP-Annotated VCF as Table

vcf_path <- "/home/akorosteleva/tutorial/example_1/Patient_2.haplotypecaller.filtered_VEP.ann.vcf"

# Read VCF as a plain table (skip header lines; adjust if needed)
vcf_data <- fread(vcf_path, skip = 3401)

# Save as CSV
csv_out <- "/home/akorosteleva/tutorial/example_1/Patient_2.vep_annotated.csv"
fwrite(vcf_data, csv_out)

head(vcf_data)

## 6: GT:AD:DP:GQ:PL    0/1:19,17:36:99:628,0,747

Task 2: Filter Damaging Variants

vcf_csv <- read.csv(csv_out)

# Filter for likely damaging/pathogenic, excluding uncertain or benign
vcf_damaging <- vcf_csv %>%
  filter(grepl("damaging|pathogenic|High Impact", INFO, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(!str_detect(INFO, regex("uncertain_significance|benign", ignore_case = TRUE)))

dim(vcf_damaging)
## [1]  4 10

Task 3: Visualize Variant Consequences

Note: VEP writes the CSQ header in the VCF header (not read by fread here).
In this tutorial we assume the second CSQ field corresponds to Consequence.

# Extract CSQ field entries (comma-separated transcript annotations)
extract_csq_entries <- function(info_str) {
  parts <- strsplit(info_str, ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
  csq_entry <- grep("^CSQ=", parts, value = TRUE)
  if (length(csq_entry) == 0) return(character(0))
  strsplit(sub("^CSQ=", "", csq_entry), ",", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
}

# From each CSQ entry, take field #2 = Consequence (pipe-separated fields)
extract_consequence <- function(csq_entry) {
  fields <- strsplit(csq_entry, "\\|")[[1]]
  if (length(fields) >= 2) fields[2] else NA_character_
}

csq_data <- vcf_damaging$INFO %>%
  lapply(extract_csq_entries) %>%
  unlist() %>%
  lapply(extract_consequence) %>%
  unlist()

csq_df <- data.frame(Consequence = csq_data) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Consequence)) %>%
  group_by(Consequence) %>%
  summarise(N = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(N))

ggplot(csq_df, aes(x = reorder(Consequence, -N), y = N)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Damaging Consequences", x = "Consequence", y = "Count")

Why we have 10 variants and 11 consequence annotations?

Some variants may have multiple consequence annotations in the CSQ field (e.g., multiple transcripts), so one variant can contribute more than one consequence.

Task 4: Investigate NM_001083116.3(PRF1):c.1034C>G (p.Pro345Arg) Variant [Patient 2]

You may look at other patients (23, 25) in github folder.

# Search for variants annotated with PRF1 in the INFO column
prf1_variants <- vcf_csv %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("PRF1", INFO, ignore.case = TRUE))

# Display matching variants
prf1_variants %>%
  dplyr::select(X.CHROM, POS, REF, ALT, INFO) %>%
  head(20)

## 3 Task 5: Compare [Patient 2] HaplotypeCaller vs DeepVariant

We compare the two callers using practical technical parameters from the VCF:

  1. Total variants and PASS rate
  2. Overlap / concordance (shared vs unique calls)
  3. FILTER distribution
  4. QUAL distribution
  5. FORMAT-derived metrics (when present): DP, GQ, VAF (from AD)
  6. Optional: same comparison restricted to a gene of interest (e.g., PRF1)

5.1 Load both callsets

read_vcf_table <- function(path){
  lines <- readLines(path)

  # Find the header line starting with #CHROM
  header_line <- grep("^#CHROM", lines)

  if(length(header_line) == 0){
    stop("Could not find #CHROM header line.")
  }

  df <- fread(path, skip = header_line - 1)

  return(df)}

library(data.table)
library(dplyr)

hc_path <- "/home/akorosteleva/tutorial/example_1/Patient_2.haplotypecaller.filtered_VEP.ann.vcf"
dv_path <- "/home/akorosteleva/tutorial/example_1/Patient_2.deepvariant_VEP.ann.vcf"

hc <- read_vcf_table(hc_path)
dv <- read_vcf_table(dv_path)

names(hc)
##  [1] "#CHROM"              "POS"                 "ID"                 
##  [4] "REF"                 "ALT"                 "QUAL"               
##  [7] "FILTER"              "INFO"                "FORMAT"             
## [10] "Patient_2_Patient_2"
names(dv)
##  [1] "#CHROM"              "POS"                 "ID"                 
##  [4] "REF"                 "ALT"                 "QUAL"               
##  [7] "FILTER"              "INFO"                "FORMAT"             
## [10] "Patient_2_Patient_2"
make_key <- function(df){
  df %>%
    mutate(var_key = paste(`#CHROM`, POS, REF, ALT, sep=":"))
}



hc <- make_key(hc)
dv <- make_key(dv)

# Ensure QUAL numeric
hc$QUAL <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(hc$QUAL))
dv$QUAL <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dv$QUAL))

head(hc)
##    #CHROM      POS          ID    REF    ALT    QUAL FILTER
##    <char>    <int>      <char> <char> <char>   <num> <char>
## 1:   chr1 11029957  rs79393073      A      G  157.64   PASS
## 2:   chr1 11030230  rs61735593      C      T 1689.64   PASS
## 3:   chr1 11031148  rs17409276      G      A  250.97   PASS
## 4:   chr1 11034700  rs70977538      G     GA  475.60   PASS
## 5:   chr1 11034982   rs6695096      C      T  914.64   PASS
## 6:   chr1 11037490 rs116685064      C      A  620.64   PASS
##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        INFO
##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      <char>

##                FORMAT               Patient_2_Patient_2            var_key
##                <char>                            <char>             <char>
## 1: GT:GQ:DP:AD:VAF:PL               0/1:2:2:0,2:1:3,0,0   chr1:2347542:C:T
## 2: GT:GQ:DP:AD:VAF:PL     0/1:16:6:1,5:0.833333:44,0,15  chr1:11029957:A:G
## 3: GT:GQ:DP:AD:VAF:PL 0/1:48:126:58,68:0.539683:49,0,52  chr1:11030230:C:T
## 4: GT:GQ:DP:AD:VAF:PL            1/1:14:6:0,6:1:39,13,0  chr1:11031148:G:A
## 5: GT:GQ:DP:AD:VAF:PL      0/1:31:75:31,33:0.44:30,0,49 chr1:11034700:G:GA
## 6: GT:GQ:DP:AD:VAF:PL  0/0:46:91:75,13:0.142857:0,49,49  chr1:11034733:T:A

5.2 Variant counts, PASS rate, overlap

basic_stats <- function(df, caller_name){
  tibble(
    caller = caller_name,
    n_variants = n_distinct(df$var_key),
    pass_variants = sum(df$FILTER == "PASS", na.rm = TRUE),
    pass_rate = mean(df$FILTER == "PASS", na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

stats_tbl <- bind_rows(
  basic_stats(hc, "HaplotypeCaller"),
  basic_stats(dv, "DeepVariant")
)

stats_tbl
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##   caller          n_variants pass_variants pass_rate
##   <chr>                <int>         <int>     <dbl>
## 1 HaplotypeCaller       2340          2335     0.998
## 2 DeepVariant           3031          2344     0.773
shared_keys <- intersect(hc$var_key, dv$var_key)
hc_unique   <- setdiff(hc$var_key, dv$var_key)
dv_unique   <- setdiff(dv$var_key, hc$var_key)

tibble(
  shared = length(shared_keys),
  haplotypecaller_only = length(hc_unique),
  deepvariant_only = length(dv_unique)
)
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   shared haplotypecaller_only deepvariant_only
##    <int>                <int>            <int>
## 1   2279                   61              752

5.3 FILTER distribution

hc_filter <- hc %>% count(FILTER, sort = TRUE) %>% mutate(caller = "HaplotypeCaller")
dv_filter <- dv %>% count(FILTER, sort = TRUE) %>% mutate(caller = "DeepVariant")

bind_rows(hc_filter, dv_filter) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = FILTER, y = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~caller, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "FILTER distribution", x = "FILTER", y = "Count")

5.4 QUAL distribution

qual_df <- bind_rows(
  hc %>% transmute(caller = "HaplotypeCaller", QUAL),
  dv %>% transmute(caller = "DeepVariant", QUAL)
) %>% filter(!is.na(QUAL))

ggplot(qual_df, aes(x = QUAL)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  facet_wrap(~caller, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "QUAL distribution", x = "QUAL", y = "Variants")

Is something looks wrong in this distribution?

5.5 Parse FORMAT metrics (DP, GQ, AD -> VAF)

### 5.5 Parse FORMAT metrics (DP, GQ, AD -> VAF)

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)

# Robustly detect the CHROM column name
get_chrom_colname <- function(df){
  chrom_candidates <- c("#CHROM","CHROM","X.CHROM","X..CHROM","X.CHROM.")
  chrom_col <- intersect(chrom_candidates, names(df))[1]
  if (is.na(chrom_col)) {
    stop("Cannot find CHROM column. Available columns are: ", paste(names(df), collapse = ", "))
  }
  chrom_col
}

# Robustly detect the sample column name (assumes 1-sample VCF)
get_sample_colname <- function(df){
  known <- c("#CHROM","CHROM","X.CHROM","X..CHROM","X.CHROM.",
             "POS","ID","REF","ALT","QUAL","FILTER","INFO","FORMAT","var_key")
  candidates <- setdiff(names(df), known)
  if (length(candidates) == 0) stop("No sample column detected. Check your VCF table columns.")
  tail(candidates, 1)
}

parse_format_metrics <- function(df){
  chrom_col  <- get_chrom_colname(df)
  sample_col <- get_sample_colname(df)

  # Split FORMAT tags (GT:AD:DP:GQ:...)
  fmt_tags <- strsplit(as.character(df$FORMAT), ":", fixed = TRUE)

  # Split sample values using the same separator
  sample_vals <- strsplit(as.character(df[[sample_col]]), ":", fixed = TRUE)

  parsed <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(df)), function(i){
    tags <- fmt_tags[[i]]
    vals <- sample_vals[[i]]

    if (length(vals) < length(tags)) {
      vals <- c(vals, rep(NA, length(tags) - length(vals)))
    }

    out <- as.list(vals[seq_along(tags)])
    names(out) <- tags
    out
  })

  parsed_df <- bind_rows(parsed)

  # Keep key variant columns + parsed FORMAT fields
  out <- bind_cols(
    df %>%
      transmute(
        var_key,
        CHROM = .data[[chrom_col]],
        POS, REF, ALT, QUAL, FILTER, INFO
      ),
    parsed_df
  )

  # Convert numeric fields if present
  if ("DP" %in% names(out)) out$DP <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(out$DP))
  if ("GQ" %in% names(out)) out$GQ <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(out$GQ))

  # Compute VAF from AD if present (ref,alt)
  if ("AD" %in% names(out)) {
    ad_split <- strsplit(as.character(out$AD), ",", fixed = TRUE)
    out$AD_ref <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(sapply(ad_split, function(x) if(length(x)>=1) x[1] else NA)))
    out$AD_alt <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(sapply(ad_split, function(x) if(length(x)>=2) x[2] else NA)))
    out$VAF <- out$AD_alt / (out$AD_ref + out$AD_alt)
  }

  out
}

hc_m <- parse_format_metrics(hc) %>% mutate(caller = "HaplotypeCaller")
dv_m <- parse_format_metrics(dv) %>% mutate(caller = "DeepVariant")

metrics <- bind_rows(hc_m, dv_m)

# Show which technical FORMAT fields were found
setdiff(names(metrics), c("var_key","CHROM","POS","REF","ALT","QUAL","FILTER","INFO","caller"))
## [1] "GT"     "AD"     "DP"     "GQ"     "PL"     "AD_ref" "AD_alt" "VAF"

5.6 Compare DP, GQ, VAF distributions

if ("DP" %in% names(metrics)) {
  ggplot(metrics %>% filter(!is.na(DP)), aes(x = DP)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
    facet_wrap(~caller, scales = "free_y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Depth (DP) distribution", x = "DP", y = "Variants")
}

if ("GQ" %in% names(metrics)) {
  ggplot(metrics %>% filter(!is.na(GQ)), aes(x = GQ)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
    facet_wrap(~caller, scales = "free_y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Genotype Quality (GQ) distribution", x = "GQ", y = "Variants")
}

if ("VAF" %in% names(metrics)) {
  ggplot(metrics %>% filter(!is.na(VAF)), aes(x = VAF)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
    facet_wrap(~caller, scales = "free_y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Variant Allele Fraction (VAF) distribution (from AD)", x = "VAF", y = "Variants")
}

5.7 Shared vs caller-unique variants

label_shared_unique <- function(df_metrics, shared_keys){
  df_metrics %>% mutate(set = ifelse(var_key %in% shared_keys, "Shared", "Caller-unique"))
}

hc_su <- label_shared_unique(hc_m, shared_keys)
dv_su <- label_shared_unique(dv_m, shared_keys)

su <- bind_rows(hc_su, dv_su)

ggplot(su %>% filter(!is.na(QUAL)), aes(x = QUAL)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  facet_grid(caller ~ set, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "QUAL: shared vs caller-unique variants", x = "QUAL", y = "Variants")

if ("DP" %in% names(su)) {
  ggplot(su %>% filter(!is.na(DP)), aes(x = DP)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
    facet_grid(caller ~ set, scales = "free_y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "DP: shared vs caller-unique variants", x = "DP", y = "Variants")
}

if ("VAF" %in% names(su)) {
  ggplot(su %>% filter(!is.na(VAF)), aes(x = VAF)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
    facet_grid(caller ~ set, scales = "free_y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "VAF: shared vs caller-unique variants", x = "VAF", y = "Variants")
}

5.8 Optional: Focus on a gene (e.g., PRF1)

gene_of_interest <- "PRF1"

hc_gene <- hc_m %>% filter(grepl(gene_of_interest, INFO, ignore.case = TRUE))
dv_gene <- dv_m %>% filter(grepl(gene_of_interest, INFO, ignore.case = TRUE))

tibble(
  gene = gene_of_interest,
  hc_variants = n_distinct(hc_gene$var_key),
  dv_variants = n_distinct(dv_gene$var_key),
  shared = length(intersect(hc_gene$var_key, dv_gene$var_key))
)
## # A tibble: 1 × 4
##   gene  hc_variants dv_variants shared
##   <chr>       <int>       <int>  <int>
## 1 PRF1           10          10     10

Notes for interpretation

  • Shared variants are typically higher confidence (higher QUAL / higher DP).
  • Caller-unique variants often occur at low coverage, low genotype quality, or harder regions.
  • If a clinically relevant variant is present in only one callset, check: FILTER, QUAL, DP, GQ, and AD/VAF (if available).
